title: Contact - Satvir Kaur MP
meta_description: Have a question, comment or enquiry? Get in touch with Satvir and share your views via the contact form on this page.
text_excerpt: Contact - Satvir Kaur MP Skip to the content Satvir Kaur MP Fighting for Southampton Menu Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Menu Close Menu Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns .sub-menu" data-toggle-type="slidetoggle" data-toggle-duration="250" aria-expanded="false"> Show sub menu Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Home How I Can Help In Parliament Campaigns .sub-menu" data-toggle-type="slidetoggle" data-toggle-duration="250" aria-expanded="false"> Show sub menu Bring back a police front counter in Shirley About Me Values News Contact Facebook Twitter Instagram Contact Please do get in touch with me and my team if there is anything I can ever assist you with. Whether it be giving your opinion on policy, sharing ideas about making Southampton a better place, or asking for help in hard times, my door is always open and my first priority is serving you. Please note, strict Parliamentary rules say that MPs can only help their own constituents. Please visit this website and enter your postcode to check if I am your MP. Please make sure you have included your full name, address and contact details in your correspondence. Contact Details Postal Address: Satvir Kaur MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA Email: satvir.kaur.mp@parliament.uk Phone: 02382 546 357 🗞️ SIGN UP TO MY NEWSLETTER 🗞️ A note about acceptable communication I receive hundreds of emails from constituents each day and my constituency office staff speak to many people over the phone every week. I also interact with lots of people via my social media pages and in Facebook groups. Whilst the vast majority of communication is civil and respectful, even if we are in profound disagreement, occasionally an email, phone conversation, or social media comment can tip from being impassioned to being disrespectful or even abusive. In these instances – particularly when racist, sexist, homophobic, personal, offensive or derogatory language is used – I will take a zero-tolerance approach. The wellbeing of my staff is my utmost priority and I reserve the right to cease communication with those concerned, whether by ending a phone call, not replying to an email, hiding or deleting social media posts or comments, or blocking repeat offenders. Thank you so much to all who contact me, I really appreciate hearing views from across the political spectrum and think there is huge value in respectful debate. This is just a small plea, from me and my team, to make sure we keep it respectful, for the benefit of all.
